content:
Today’s 9to5Toys Lunch Break is headlined by all 4 colors of Apple’s most affordable new 1TB M5 MacBook Air back at $150 off today alongside the new 2TB M5 Pro MacBook Pro hitting its best Amazon price ever at over $160 off and the ongoing all-time low on the entry 2026 16-inch M5 Pro MacBook Pro. You can score some of Apple’s elusive black USB-C cables from $7, the Twelve South PowerBug wall charger with Qi2 MagSafe has dropped even lower to a new $35 Amazon all-time low, and AirPods Pro 3 are still on sale at the $200 holiday pricing. Head below for a closer look at the details.

All 4 colors of Apple’s most affordable new 1TB M5 MacBook Air are back at $150 off today

As we mentioned yesterday, some of the M5 MacBook Air deals are quickly jumping back up in price as of the start of this week. While it seemed as though all of the more affordable 13-inch models were headed in that direction, Amazon is once again offering the mid-tier 16GB/1TB model in all four colors back at $150 off this morning.

Amazon is offering Apple’s new 2026 13-inch M5 MacBook Air with the 16GB of RAM and 1TB storage down at $1,149 shipped. This includes the Midnight, Silver, Starlight, and Sky Blue models at the time of writing.

While there is still a chance we see these configs start to head back closer to full price, much the same as the more affordable 16GB/512GB model deals that are all at higher prices now, we are indeed seeing the straight $150 price drop on all colors at the moment.

This is a regularly $1,299 config that is now back at the lowest prices we have tracked since release in early March of this year.

There are also some other lingering $150 price drops across the lineup for folks looking towards the 24GB variants or the larger 15-inch form-factors:

  • 13-inch M5 MacBook Air 16GB/1TB from $1,149 (Reg. $1,299)
  • 13-inch M5 MacBook Air 24GB/1TB from $1,349 (Reg. $1,499)
  • 15-inch M5 MacBook Air 16GB/512GB from $1,149 (Reg. $1,299)
  • 15-inch M5 MacBook Air 16GB/1TB from $1,349 (Reg. $1,499)
  • 15-inch M5 MacBook Air 24GB/1TB from $1,549 (Reg. $1,699)

Apple’s new 2TB M5 Pro MacBook Pro just hit its best Amazon price ever at over $160 off

We have spotted some particularly notable deals on MacBook Pro models this week, including this M5 at the best price of the year and a new all-time low on Apple’s least expensive 2026 16-inch M5 Pro MacBook Pro. This morning, however, we are also adding Amazon’s best price to date on the 24GB/2TB M5 Pro MacBook Pro as well.

Amazon is now offering the 14-inch M5 Pro MacBook Pro with 24GB of RAM and the 2TB storage capacity starting at $2,438.50 shipped – this is the 15-core CPU/16-core GPU model.

This is a regularly $2,599 configuration that is now $161 off the list price to deliver the best deal we have spotted on Amazon for a 2TB M5 Pro MacBook Pro since release last month. As of right now only the Space Black model is down this low – the Silver model is selling for $2,533 (don’t buy this one at that price).

Best Buy is charging full price for both colorways right now.

This is the first time we have tracked any of the new 14-inch 2026 M5 Pro models at better than $150 off the list price. The 16-inch with 24GB of RAM and the 1TB SSD, however, is currently sitting all-time low pricing as well at $200 off the list, for the record.

  • 14-inch M5 Pro MacBook Pro 24GB/1TB $2,049 (Reg. $2,199)
  • 16-inch M5 Pro MacBook Pro 24GB/2TB $2,499 (Reg. $2,699) – new all-time low at $200 off
  • 16-inch M5 Pro MacBook Pro 48GB/1TB $2,899 (Reg. $3,099)
  • 14-inch M5 Max MacBook Pro 36GB/2TB $3,449 (Reg. $3,599)
  • 16-inch M5 Max MacBook Pro 36GB/2TB $3,699 (Reg. $3,899)
  • 16-inch M5 Max MacBook Pro 48GB/2TB $4,200 (Reg. $4,399)

Apple’s least expensive 2026 16-inch M5 Pro MacBook Pro just hit its best price ever at $200 off

Score Apple’s elusive black USB-C cables from $7 each while you still can

Now even lower: Twelve South’s PowerBug wall charger with Qi2 MagSafe hits $35 Amazon all-time low

Update 4/21: The price has now dropped again to a new $35.05 Amazon low.

Amazon is now offering its best price to date on the Twelve South PowerBug – a handy gadget that transforms any outlet into a MagSafe charger. Regularly $50, you’ll now find the black/slate model sitting down at a new $39 Amazon all-time low.

Today’s accessories and charging deals:

  • Anker 140W 4-port Laptop Charger with smart display is down to $60 low today
  • Don’t care about the latest model? Logitech’s MX Master 2S Ergonomic Bluetooth mouse is down to just $52
  • Organize your tech ahead of your next trip with tomtoc’s essentials bag, now $19
  • Sharge’s new HyperTower 170W 25,000mAh display-equipped power bank debuts with launch deal & FREE USB-C cable
  • Anker 6-outlet Power Strip with USB-C and Surge Protection at $20 today
  • Anker’s Nano 75W car charger with retractable USB-C cable just hit a new all-time low at $20
  • Belkin’s new 9,300mAh wireless charging camera grip with MagSafe/Pixelsnap support is down to just $64 today

Spring Apple Watch deals disappearing, score the 46mm model at $100 off while you still can

While those rare Apple Watch Ultra 3 deals we were tracking last week have come and gone now, the most affordable 46mm Apple Watch Series 11 models are still $100 off at Amazon.

Amazon is now offering the base model Apple Watch Series 11 down at $329 shipped right now. This includes the Jet Black, Space Gray, and Silver cases – the Rose Gold has jumped up $20 right now.

The deals on the smaller 42mm model have almost entirely dried up now as only the Rose Gold model is still at the $100 off range.

While we have caught the more pricey GPS + Cell models, including both he aluminum and Titanium models at better than $100 off a couple times now, the base, and most popular GPS-only variants are yet to drop any lower than the $100 off mark. All things considered, they are only about half way through their yearly lifecycle and landing one at $100 off is still a solid discount nonetheless.

If you’re looking to nab one before the summer hits, the deals look to be on the way out at this point so you might want to jump in there sooner than later if the 46mm is what you need – we do not recommend purchasing a base Apple Watch Series 11 until it is at least $99 off at this point.
